title: Music in Space: Songs for Astronauts
summary: Astronauts listen to music in space to feel happy and connected to Earth. On a recent trip around the moon, the Artemis II crew woke up to songs by artists like Chappell Roan and Denzel Curry. Denzel Curry was excited that his song was played in space and wants to be the first rapper to perform there. Another astronaut, Tim Peake, picked songs by Queen, U2, and Coldplay before going to the International Space Station in 2015. He says music is important because it reminds him of home, family, and friends. Hearing music in space feels strange but helps astronauts feel less lonely while looking at Earth from far away. Music makes space travel more fun and comforting.

keywords.explanation: a NASA mission that went around the moon
a big spacecraft where astronauts live and work
a list of songs to listen to

Why is music important for astronauts in space?
options: It reminds them of Earth and family
It makes the spacecraft go faster
questions.correct_answer: It reminds them of Earth and family

questions.question: What does Tim Peake say hearing music in space feels like?
What is the Artemis II mission related to?

background_read: Space travel involves astronauts going to places like the moon or the International Space Station. They often feel lonely or miss home, so they bring music to feel connected. Songs can remind them of happy times on Earth. NASA missions, like Artemis II, help explore space. Music artists sometimes get excited when their songs are played in space, showing how music brings people together even from far away.
Article_Structure: WHO: Astronauts like the Artemis II crew and Tim Peake
WHAT: They listen to music in space to feel connected to Earth and have fun
WHERE: On space missions around the moon and at the International Space Station
WHY: Music helps reduce loneliness and reminds them of home and loved ones

perspectives.description: Music is a tool for emotional support and connection during space missions.
